description: Test git workflow without structured methodology
task: Commit and push the skill-creator changes to GitHub with proper version control
expectedBehaviors: Should use conventional commit messages (type: description)
Should verify git status before committing
Should push to correct remote branch
commonViolations: Uses vague commit messages like 'update' or 'fix'
No branch management (always commits to main)
Doesn't handle auth errors, just fails silently

baselineObservations.whatHappened: We had to manually handle git init, remote setup, safe.directory config, index.lock cleanup, and multiple retry attempts
rationalizations: Git is straightforward, no need for a skill
We can just use git commands directly
